calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Final Distribution/Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-17-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-16-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Franceschini for and with Bernard Ryan.
Allison Bellumori appeared by telephone for Brandenberger & Davis (Assignee).
Thomas Cain appeared by telephone for Shari Curet (Interested Party/Heir).
Andrew Cleland (Claimant) appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Mr. Franceschini informed the Court that he filed an amended petition on March 29, 2017 addressing all of the Examiner's notes (copy provided to the Court). Mr. Cain stated that he supports the petition as amended. Ms. Bellumori stated that her Client does not object. Mr. Cleland stated that he has no objection. The Petition for Settlement of First and Final Account, etc. is taken under submission (subject to receiving and reviewing the amended proposed order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Off Calendar Feb-21-2017 Continued To Mar-21-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Heirship (Probate Code Section 11700)
Off Calendar Jul-12-2016 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Determine Heirship (Probate Code Section 11700)
Off Calendar May-25-2016 Continued To Jul-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Julia Wald for and with (administrator) Bernard Ryan. Andrew Cleland (via court call) in Propria Persona. Allison Bellumori (via court call) for Brandenberger & Davis. Thomas Cain (via court call) for (petitioner) Shari Curet.

The Court asked where we stand. Ms. Wald reported that they hired an investigation firm who is doing research to locate the father, that they have received threatening letters from Mr. Cain and the Legacy Law Group, that they have gone ahead with the research, that the firm is waiting for answers from Social Security and another agency, that they are currently under budget and that the mother's side claims that the father's side cannot inherit as he was not a part of the decedent's life. The Court asked if there was any timeline by the agency. Ms. Wald stated that they were told they would know by now, that it is dependent on Social Security getting back to them, that if they find the father they will then need to look for heirs. The Court asked if Social Security has communicated anything to the investigators as to where they are and was told no. Ms. Bellamori stated that there is an additional requirement of clear and convincing evidence and that DNA would be needed. The Court stated that we are not at that stage yet, that the father needs to be found first. Mr. Cain stated that there is a misconception, that they have done the search, that they welcome all heirs, that there is no reasonable likelihood that they will find other heirs and that they need a clear cutoff. Mr. Cleland stated that on 4/22/16 Ms. Wald issued a letter regarding a continuance because they had suspended the search. The Court stated that we know why she is asking for more time. Ms. Wald stated that they were threatened that Mr. Ryan would be surcharged and they had to research that possibility. The Court stated that it is the Court's view that it is proper to do this search. The petition to determine heirship was continued to July 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that it would be good to know ahead of time what has been found.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determine Heirship (Probate Code Section 11700)
Off Calendar May-11-2016 Continued To May-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Heirship (Probate Code Section 11700)
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Continued To May-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Cain for (petitioner) Shari Curet. Julia Wald for and with (administrator) Bernard Ryan. Andrew Cleland (via court call) in Propria Persona. Allison Bellumori (via court call) for Brandenburger & Davis.

The Court stated that the administrator has requested additional time to follow up on leads on heirs on the father's side of the family. Mr. Cain stated that they object and that there has been sufficient due diligence. The Court asked if counsel has spoken with the possible heirs. Mr. Cain stated that he has not, that they have a series of facts and that their research finds that the father cannot be found. The Court stated that this is not a hearing to determine if they are the heirs or not and that the administrator is just looking for more time. Mr. Cain stated that he would encourage it if they were real leads, that it is just speculation, that there is no evidence to move forward and that they are not going to establish an heir based on someone who died in 1927. Ms. Bellumori stated that she agrees with Mr. Cain, that the legal standard is not there and that a continued search is a waste of the estates money. Mr. Cleland stated that there is no father listed on the birth certificate. Ms. Wald stated that the problem is that those arguing stand to lose if heirs of the father are found, that Mr. Cleland did have a father, that the father's line has some rights to the money and that it would not be right to not pursue further. The petition to determine heirship was continued to May 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to give the administrator an opportunity to follow up on leads. The Court stated that it understands the objectors reasons to cut off the search but cannot conclude that there has been due diligence when the administrator has come up with more leads.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Jan-12-2016 Continued To Feb-08-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-22-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Julia Pool Wald, for and with Petitioner Bernard Ryan

Ms. Wald stated that she was not aware of any objection to the amended petition for letters of administration. The Court stated that the only question he had was to ascertain that there was no request for authority to act under the IAEA. Ms. Wald confirmed that no such authority was being requested. The Court granted the Amended Petition for Letters of Administration (order signed). Bond in the amount of $2,080,000.00 is required. The Coyurt set a Status Hearing re Final Distribution/Closing of Estate for July 17, 2017 at 9:00 am in department 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Continued To Jul-22-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Julia Wald for and with Bernard Ryan. Steve Weinkauf for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Weinkauf stated that his clients agreed that Mr. Ryan's petition has priority and that they had no objection to it going forward. The court stated that there is a procedural issue and that the amended petition will need to be re-published, that a notice of the amended petition needs to be filed and that it is not clear what is meant by "alleged relatives" in item 8 of the petition. Ms. Wald stated that she had been contacted by attorneys and companies that stated they represented heirs and that they had been noticed and that they are hopeful that heirs will come forward. The court stated that it is the responsibility of the administrator to make a determination of who the heirs are and that the word "alleged" suggests ambiguity. Ms. Wald stated that it is their personal information that there is no one related to the decedent. The petition for letters of administration was continued to July 22, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
